title: GodDARN 20 is silly
wikitext:
[]: GodDARN, 2.0 is silly. [Spoilers, yes, there are.]             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:37 am The first 25 minutes or so, anyway, are relentlessly over-the-top in terms of silliness, slapstick, and wacky, kooky zaniness. There's nothing intrinsically wrong with being goofy, but 1.0 was pretty toned down in terms of jocularity compared to NGE. The loony antics have been knocked up several notches in 2.0 , however, to the point that I often wanted it to just calm the heck down . Frequent cartoony expressions and actions, along with nearly incessant campy music, made it really, really hard to take anything seriously. For example: -The English dialogue at the beginning sounded like they got the bottom of the 4Kids barrel to record it. I mean it's original Resident Evil bad. I remember Ornette saying that the English acting was good... What? Still, I'm sure that's not intentional. I'm guessing most Japanese don't know what makes for natural-sounding English. -Clockiel looked and acted fantastic. The best Angel up to that point. But its entrance and Asuka's were guano crazy . They don't even have anything remotely approaching a proper battle worthy of the awesomeness of either party until Asuka dropkicks its core out in the nuttiest move an Eva has performed to date. Nuttier than dancing, even. -This brings me to Misato's car spinning out of control and landing at the Eva's foot WHILE SHE AND Shinji ARE STILL INSIDE IT. That's FLCL stuff right there. If something like that happened in 1.0 it would seem unforgivably out of place. Here, everything's so cuckoo that the sporadic serious elements at the beginning seem out of place. -Nebuchadnezzar's Key being introduced in the exact same way as Adam probably wasn't supposed to make me laugh, but the way the movie was going up to that point, it seemed like a deadpan joke and I giggled when Gendo announced what he had received. -Misato's sniffing against the glass with the "Dah! Da dahhh! Dah!" chorus was facepalmworthy. Pretty much all the music that wasn't used for battles (and some of the battle music) was silly, full of overblown ROCK N' ROLL YEAAAHHH! guitar, and basically the type of thing that made what I was watching feel like a mindless action film. -When Asuka moved in, they combined Misato's already cartoony SUPRISE appearance with the silliest facial expressions to ever grace Evangelion . Asuka's variation on the toothpick scene with the straw barely covering her nipples was hilarious, but really frustrating for a film series already full of nipples. -And I feel this deserves its own separate bullet for how much it killed me inside to see a naked Asuka kick Shinji in the face, followed by a twitching, drooling Shinji. That's straight out of Girlfriend of Steel there, guys, and I hate to say that I hate it, but I hate it. I can't take this thing seriously. I'm really, really trying, but then Asuka goes and kicks another guy in the face. Blalaaaauuaahrpppp. -PenPen drinking beer and sleeping with his head stuck in Misato's shirt is daffy. -The decontamination scene is bonkers. -PenPen talking to the other penguins and dancing around the picnic is just... well, at least he doesn't have a gold tooth and speak ebonics. -The Matariel fight was really well done, but Unit 01 doing Sonic the Hedgehog's classic "GOTTA GO FAST !" pose as she ran up ramps and jumped across rising buildings took away from the coolness. I mean, with all the complaints about Transformers 2 being overblown and silly, it's pretty impossible to defend Evangelion at this point. So it gets less silly from that point, I guess, but by then the damage is done. Often having lighthearted screwball comedy before drama heightens the tension of the drama, but the first part of 2.0 just overdoses the bejeezers out of its supply of apewire and, following the generally serious and solemn 1.0 and preceding the generally serious and solemn but still over the top rest of the movie, it's... well, it's preposterous.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:48 am Have you forgotten the action arc of the original series.I mean "Dance Like You Want to Win" was goofier than anything 2.0 done.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:51 am Lagann wrote: Have you forgotten the action arc of the original series.I mean "Dance Like You Want to Win" was goofier than anything 2.0 done.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:04 am NAveryW wrote: GodDARN, 2.0 is silly.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:09 am NAveryW wrote: Lagann wrote: Have you forgotten the action arc of the original series.I mean "Dance Like You Want to Win" was goofier than anything 2.0 done.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:21 am I'm not going to deny that the action arc could get pretty silly, but it wasn't relentlessly so. Once Mari's first scene is over, 2.0 goes Looney Tunes and stays that way for around twenty minutes without much breathing room between anvil drops. Oh yes, and Misato's apathetic reaction to Shinji being kicked into what is obviously excruciating, immobilizing pain seems reeeeally out of character when you look at her reaction in 1.0 to Shinji being bullied at school. If you're a child's guardian and you see him kicked in the face and flop down on the floor twitching, moaning and drooling, if your reaction is to help the poor boy back on his feet, you have at least minimal competence. If your reaction is to smile and talk to your penguin, not so much as asking the boy if he is in need of medical attention, you either 1) are a horrible, horrible guardian who would hopefully care more if you weren't so inebriated, or 2) live in a world where people get bonked on the heads with sledge hammers and shake the circle of dancing stars away with a "yotta-yotta-yotta" sound effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:27 am NAveryW wrote: 2.0 goes Looney Tunes

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:01 am http://projectenthalpy.com/shinjifacekick.png

[]:             Quote Post by Ingrod » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:27 am OK, 2.0 is comedic in the part that original series was comedic, and that is silly... now, ok. I dont see contradiction with first reviews, perhaps because the reviewer dont see that blantantly silly how you. Also, is Tsurumaki, FLCL and Diebuster autor, the person under the combat scenes, what you expect?

[]:             Quote Post by OKdesuKA » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:54 am You're exaggerating.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:56 am OKdesuKA wrote: You're exaggerating.

[]:             Quote Post by OKdesuKA » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:02 am Lagann wrote: Have you forgotten the action arc of the original series.I mean "Dance Like You Want to Win" was goofier than anything 2.0 done.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:07 am Nothing in "Dance Like You Want to Win" defied NGE's own internal logic. Kaji suggested the dance and, though crazy (and Misato stated it was crazy), the idea made sense. It's a great mnemonic device as well as a synchronization tactic. Ending up upside down was cartoony, yes. So was PenPen falling over from eating Misato's terrible food. So was Asuka's face and the accompanying sound effect when Hikari said she liked Toji's "sensitive side". I still don't think that comes close to the mayhem shown in 2.0 .

[]:             Quote Post by OKdesuKA » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:12 am Are you some kind of troll? Either way I don't see the difference. Then again I always fail to remember one small quote: There are always gonna be people unsatisfied with adaptations from one medium to another. Learn that before you ever make a criticism of a criticism ever again.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:15 am OKdesuKA wrote: Enjoy being dissatisfied

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:43 am OKdesuKA wrote: There are always gonna be people unsatisfied with adaptations from one medium to another. Learn that before you ever make a criticism of a criticism ever again.

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:49 am Silliness? In 2.0?? Zeruel pulled a "Saunich der Hejhaug" on Unit-00. Argument closed. =| Yeah google that if you don't know what I'm talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 16, 2009 5:07 am Anno has trolled us yet again. Long live ubertroll Anno!

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Thu Jul 16, 2009 7:10 am Definitely add Toji being all over his sister to the list. -_- Also stuff like Rei looking all scary at the knife - up to Bardiel* at least I really had to wonder sometimes if this isn't meant to be more of a parody of the original rather than a remake/-imagining. (Not sure if the Sephiroth being used for the water-cleaning thing was meant to be an in-joke too or actually have a symbolic meaning... >_>) *what am I saying? Already forgot about GAR Shinji and other Zeruel-fight silliness.

[]:             Quote Post by Barong » Thu Jul 16, 2009 7:43 am As if the original hadn't have hours worth of silliness, but that's NGE we're talking about here, where even on the most senseless of scenes new religions were built upon. At least Anno seems to be achieving his goal of making it more easily accessible to the masses.
